data.title: Peet Buys Back 25,000 Ordinary Shares; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Peet bought back 25,000 ordinary shares for AU$32,875, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The real estate developer still has 2.7 million shares that it can repurchase under its buyback program, per the filing.

The company's shares were down almost 3% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Australian technology stocks rebound on Wall Street gains
data.description:
** Australian technology stocks rise as much as 1.5% to 2366.3, gaining most since March 3

** The sub-index gains after two straight sessions of losses

** Shares tracking overnight Wall street gains, as investors gain confidence on U.S. inflation data amid elevated concerns over a possible recession

** Software developer Megaport is up as much as 1.8%, jumping most since March 5, last up 1.5%

** Sub-index constituent Wisetech is up 1.9%, set for its first day of gains after four straight sessions of losses

** down 14.9% this year

data.title: ASKA Pharmaceutical to Cancel 1.8 Million Treasury Shares
data.description:
ASKA Pharmaceutical Holdings said it will cancel 1.8 million treasury shares, representing 5.89% of issued stock, on March 21, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Following the cancellation, the company will have 28.8 million issued shares, with 397,395 shares remaining in treasury.

data.title: Japan Logistics Fund to Buy Back Up to 69,000 Units
data.description:
Japan Logistics Fund plans to repurchase up to 69,000 investment units, or 2.5% of issued units, for up to 6 billion yen between March 13 and June 30, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

All repurchased units will be cancelled on July 31.

data.title: Gold Edges Higher as Global Trade War Prospects Grow — Market Talk
data.description: Gold edges higher in the early Asian session. EU and Canada unveiled retaliatory tariffs against the U.S. on Wednesday, further increasing prospects of a global trade war after the U.S. imposed levies on global steel and aluminum imports. Such prospects could enhance the safe-haven appeal of the precious metal. There's uncertainty over the impact of tariffs on the U.S. economy amid talk of a recession, XS.com's Samer Hasn says in an email. Market turmoil has also deepened due to President Trump's repeated "start-and-stop" over tariffs, Hasn adds. Spot gold is 0.1% higher at $2,937.82/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: TABLE-Foreign investors net sellers of Japan shares for last week
data.description:
TOKYO, March 13 (Reuters) - Foreign investors remained netsellers of Japanese stocks for the week ending on March 8, capital flows data showed on Thursday. Foreigners sold a net 220.5 billion yen worth of shares inthe week through Mar. 8, after selling a net 708.3 billion yenin the week before that. Japanese investors sold a net 355.9 billion yen of foreignbonds in the latest week after buying a net 1,514.2 billion yenthe week before. Details of net investments were as follows (in billion yen):

FOREIGN INVESTMENT IN JAPAN: (net) stocks bonds bills March 2 - March 8 -220.5 +686.4 +1,096.6 February 23 - March 1 -708.3 +776.5 -730.2

JAPANESE INVESTMENT ABROAD: (net; a minus sign indicates net selling and inflows of funds into Japan)

stocks bonds bills March 2 - March 8 +1,257.4 -355.9 +141.7 February 23 - March 1 +626.9 +1,514.2 -140.0

Notes: - An "r" denotes a revised figure. - Bonds include beneficiary certificates. - Figures are based on contracts and are rounded. - The MOF's capital flows data can be found in English at: http://www.mof.go.jp/international_policy/reference/itn_transactions_in_securities/week.pdf

((tokyo.markets@thomsonreuters.com)(+81-3-6441-1872)) - Older weekly data are omitted if there are no revisions

data.title: Nikkei May Rise as Concerns About U.S. Inflation Ebb — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks may rise as concerns about U.S. inflation recede for now. Nikkei futures are up 0.9% at 37135 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 148.25, compared with 148.07 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Besides U.S. trade policy, investors are focusing on any developments in Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 36819.09 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1951 ET - Japanese stocks may rise as concerns about U.S. inflation recede for now. Nikkei futures are up 0.9% at 37135 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 148.25, compared with 148.07 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Besides U.S. trade policy, investors are focusing on any developments in Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 36819.09 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1853 ET - Algoma Steel Group says that tariffs on steel and aluminum will have a material and adverse effect on its finances and operations. That is because tariffs have driven steel prices below production costs for much of the industry, as well as heightened general macroeconomic uncertainty. "We remain confident that the Canadian federal and provincial governments will respond swiftly and appropriately to support the industry," CEO Michael Garcia says. "Given the deeply integrated North American supply chain, we believe rational dialogue will prevail between these close allies, restoring normal steel trade." The Canadian company is currently assessing the tariffs, adding that it is too early to estimate the full extent of their effects at this time. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1846 ET [Dow Jones]--CFRA Research analyst Angelo Zino says in a research note that he views Lip-Bu Tan's appointment as Intel's chief executive positively, citing Tan's strong industry background and previous familiarity with the company's operations. "His immediate challenges include deciding whether to maintain (Intel's) integrated structure or pursue a break-up strategy," he writes. Ultimately, the company's success depends on whether it can develop competitive products in the AI-driven data center market, defend its PC market share and address its falling stock price, Zino says. "Dialogue with the U.S. government on foundry funding will also be critical." Shares rise 11%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1811 ET - Military shipbuilder Austal's A$200 million capital raising to expand its shipyard in Mobile, Alabama, doesn't scotch hopes of EPS growth, contends Bell Potter. Analyst Daniel Laing says the dilutive affect of the equity raise and changes to Austal's capex outlook drive EPS downgrades of 7% and 15% in FY 2025 and FY 2026, respectively. Still, Bell Potter says it forecasts "substantial EPS growth in FY 2025/FY 2026/FY 2027 and we believe the reiterated FY 2025 guidance of Ebit not less than A$80 million still looks conservative." It retains a buy call on Austal's stock. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1803 ET - Alkane Resources's Boda-Kaiser project in eastern Australia has become more valuable as gold prices hover near a record high, bolstering its appeal to potential investors, says Bell Potter. A scoping study released mid last year pointed to a net present value of A$1.1 billion after tax. That was based on a gold price of A$3,500/oz. Now, however, the gold price has risen to A$4,640/oz. Analyst Bradley Watson says the spot gold price suggests a post-tax net present value of A$2.1 billion. "We think the value of Boda at current gold prices will not be lost on potential partners that are scouring the gold sector for still-undervalued opportunities," says Bell Potter, which rates Alkane a buy. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1752 ET - American Eagle Outfitters is off to a slower-than-expected start in 1Q and external factors aren't making it any easier. CEO Jay Schottenstein says the clothing and accessories company behind the American Eagle and Aerie brands is working to strengthen its top-line and reduce expenses, all while navigating "through an uncertain consumer and operating landscape." American Eagle expects a mid-single digit decline in revenue in the current quarter, which would compound on a top-line decline in 4Q. Companies across all sectors are grappling with signs that consumers' are feeling less confident in the economy and pulling back on discretionary spending amid concerns about higher prices eggs and other staples, all while tariffs threaten to raise costs further and keep inflation above desired levels. Shares fall 5.3% in aftermarket trading. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1739 ET - Ramelius Resources will increasingly need to consider M&A, says Ord Minnett, after an updated plan for the Mt Magnet gold mine in Western Australia disappointed the market. The new plan highlights a steeper production fall at Mt Magnet in FY 2026-FY 2031, analyst Paul Kaner says. Ord Minnett had previously thought the Eridanus cut-back/mill expansion could plug some of this shortfall. Now, however, it appears that Ramelius will need M&A to address this, especially if its Rebecca-Roe development is also pushed out. "As a result, the market will likely place an M&A discount on the stock, regardless of track record given the higher pricing environment (i.e. seller's market)," Ord Minnett says. The bank downgrades Ramelius to accumulate, from buy. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1731 ET - Intel appoints Lip-Bu Tan as its new chief executive, succeeding co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus. The two stepped into the role in December following Pat Gelsinger's abrupt retirement. With Tan's appointment, Zinsner will return to his role of chief financial officer and executive vice president, while Johnston Holthaus will return to her role as CEO of Intel Products. Gelsinger, at the time of his departure, was in the middle of a multiyear turnaround strategy that involved spending billions of dollars building new factories and growing a contract chip-manufacturing business. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1726 ET - Intel's new CEO Lip-Bu Tan says he respects and admires the semiconductor company, and that he sees significant opportunities for it to remake its business to better serve customers and create shareholder value. "Intel has a powerful and differentiated computing platform, a vast customer installed base and a robust manufacturing footprint that is getting stronger by the day as we rebuild our process technology roadmap," he says. "I am eager to join the company and build upon the work the entire Intel team has been doing to position our business for the future." Shares rise 12% after hours. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1720 ET - Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to pare its recent heavy losses when the local share market opens Thursday. ASX futures are up by about 0.2% ahead of the open, suggesting that the benchmark index will bounce following a session in which it briefly entered correction territory. The ASX 200 ended Wednesday 1.3% lower, 9.0% below the record close it set less than four weeks ago. Yancoal and Perpetual are among the stocks set to trade ex-dividend. Major U.S. indices provided a mixed lead for the region. The S&P 500 rose 0.5% and the Nasdaq Composite gained 1.2%, but the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.2% for a third straight decline. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1717 ET - Intel names Lip-Bu Tan as its new top executive, effective March 18. Tan, who previously served on the beleaguered semiconductor company's board of directors, has more than 20 years of semiconductor and software experience, having formerly held the top role at Cadence Design Systems. At Cadence, Tan helped turn around the company, focusing on what Intel said was customer-centric innovation that helped double revenue, expand operating margins and send its stock higher. His appointment comes as Intel's core business of making chips for personal computers and servers has been challenged amid an artificial-intelligence boom that has largely benefited rival Nvidia. Shares rise 11% in after-hours trading. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1605 ET - The S&P 500 and Nasdaq rise after inflation slowed in February, though tariffs are threatening to raise some prices in the months ahead. Big technology and communication services companies lead the advance, while consumer staples are broadly weaker. Canada and the European Union announced retaliatory tariffs after the Trump administration's 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports took effect overnight. The DJIA falls 82 points, or 0.2%, to 41350, while the S&P 500 gains 0.5% to 5599 and the Nasdaq rises 1.2% to 17648. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

data.title: Zacros Wins Government Grant for New Polarizer Film Factory
data.description:
Zacros will receive up to 4 billion yen under Japan's Large-scale Growth Investment Subsidy for Wage Increases through Labor-saving Measures, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The grant will support the construction of a three-meter-wide polarizer protective film production line at its Numata plant.

data.title: City Developments: Board to Continue to Focus on Strengthening Company's Business
City Developments: All Current Directors, Including Jennifer Duong Young, Wong Su Yen, Remain on Board
ASX Small-Cap Stocks to Watch
data.description:
Investors are likely to keep an eye on the following small-cap stocks listed on the Australian Securities Exchange.

Brazilian Critical Minerals

Brazilian Critical Minerals subsidiary Mineracao BBX do Brazil secured a permit from the Environmental Protection Institute of Amazonas to pump water for field pilot trials at the Ema project over two years.

Zinc of Ireland

Zinc of Ireland said the program of work for its Mt Clere project in Western Australia received approval from the Department of Mines, Industry Regulation and Safety.

FMR Resources

FMR Resources acquired the Goshen project in Canada through direct staking.

Sarytogan Graphite

Sarytogan Graphite said the Sherubainura River Aquifer is the most suitable water source for the Sarytogan project in Kazakhstan, according to its pre-feasibility study. Two additional wells are being drilled, and seven of the 11 existing water bores are undergoing pump testing.

Sierra Nevada Gold

Sierra Nevada Gold said rock chip sampling at the Blackhawk intermediate sulfidation epithermal project in Nevada yielded high-grade silver and copper results from the Two Shovels prospect.

SBI Holdings to Book 99.8 Billion Yen Gain from Internal Securities Sale
data.description:
SBI Holdings said it will record a 99.8 billion yen gain on the sale of operational investment securities to a subsidiary in its non-consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The gain is based on Mizuho Bank's March 11 foreign exchange quotations mid-rate.

data.title: Sony Music sues University of Southern California over social media ads
data.description:
Sony Music has sued the University of Southern California in New York federal court for allegedly using more than 170 of the label's songs in videos promoting the school's athletics program without permission.

The label's lawsuit, filed on Tuesday, cited 283 videos with songs from musicians including Michael Jackson, Britney Spears and AC/DC that USC's sports teams supposedly used in TikTok and Instagram posts without licenses. Sony Music asked for statutory copyright damages of $150,000 per song, amounting to tens of millions of dollars in damages.

USC said in a statement that it "respects the intellectual property rights of others and will respond to these allegations in court." Attorneys and spokespeople for Sony Music did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Wednesday.

Sony Music said in the lawsuit that it had warned the Los Angeles university about its unauthorized use of the label's music since 2021. The label said USC uses its music to drive social media engagement and sell sports tickets and merchandise.

"Despite having been on notice of its infringing conduct, USC has repeatedly failed to obtain licenses for its use of Sony Music sound recordings on the USC Social Media Pages, although it has acknowledged that it needs music licenses, that music licenses must be paid for, that music licenses can be expensive, and that music license requests may be denied," Sony Music said in its complaint.

Sony Music said it had discussed a potential settlement with USC until January.

The label has filed similar lawsuits against companies including Marriott, which it sued last year. Sony and the hotel chain ended their dispute later that year.

The USC case is Sony Music Entertainment v. University of Southern California, U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York, No. 1:25-cv-02042.

For Sony Music: Benjamin Semel and Kaveri Arora of Pryor Cashman

For USC: attorney information not yet available

Read more:

Sony Music sues Marriott over 100s of songs used in social-media ads

Sony Music ends US legal battle with Marriott over song use in ads

data.title: Mark Carney to Be Sworn In as Canada's Prime Minister on Friday — Update
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Mark Carney will be sworn in as Canada's 24th prime minister on Friday.

Canada's Governor General, or the King of England's representative in the country, said the ceremony would also involve the swearing-in of a new cabinet.

Carney, the former chief central banker in Canada and the U.K., handily won the leadership of the governing Liberal Party this past weekend. He will replace Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, who said in early January he was leaving the post after nearly a decade, amid poor polling results.

Carney promised a quick and seamless transition, and so far this week has met with Trudeau, Canada's top soldier, the head of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police and Ontario Premier Doug Ford. Political analysts and Liberal Party members expect Carney to call a national election shortly after taking power, to take advantage of polling momentum that suggests the governing Liberals and the Conservative Party are in a statistical tie.

At the time of Trudeau's resignation, the Conservative Party, led by populist Pierre Poilievre, held a 20-point-plus lead over the Liberals.

Carney's leadership campaign has largely repudiated Trudeau's economic agenda, arguing that the prime minister and his key aides let their focus wander from fueling long-term growth and encouraging investment. In his victory speech, Carney said he would repeal some of the more unpopular tax measures that the Trudeau government introduced.

He has vowed to cut taxes for the middle class and limit government spending and the size of the federal bureaucracy, both of which climbed sharply under Trudeau's watch.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Sparc Technologies Appoints Chairman
data.description:

The company's shares gained almost 3% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: LITALICO to Sell nCS Inc. for 800 Million Yen to Focus on Core Business
data.description:
LITALICO will sell its entire stake in nCS Inc. to E-Life Group for 800 million yen, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

nCS Inc. operates rehabilitation day services in Tokyo. The sale, expected to close on March 31, will allow LITALICO to focus on its core disability welfare services.
